ideal_answer: Use the official UMT Sialkot contact details. City Campus is at 21-A Small Industrial Estate, Shahabpura Road, Sialkot, Pakistan, and the general email is info@skt.umt.edu.pk.
The UMT Sialkot City Campus is at 21-A Small Industrial Estate, Shahabpura Road, Sialkot, Pakistan.
The handbook says the library is open seven days a week except official holidays, from 0800 to 2100 on regular days and 1000 to 1700 on Sundays.
According to the handbook, undergraduates can borrow 4 books for 14 days. Reference books and journals are not normally borrowable.
For an emergency, contact campus safety channels right away and use emergency services if needed. If the situation is urgent, call 15 or 1122 and notify OSS&V.
The handbook says participants should check their registration on the portal after enrollment. If you need more help, follow the official PRS or registrar process.
Use the Student Portal or PRS Request Management System, or email prshelpdesk@umt.edu.pk. Submit the request before the semester starts and follow the official approval process.
The handbook says final transcript clearance starts from the student portal. Log in, open FT clearance, upload documents, fill details, complete feedback, and submit the request.
As an undergraduate, you may register up to 18 credit hours in a regular semester.
If attendance drops below 75 percent, the handbook says the participant is not eligible for the final exam and receives an SA grade.
Yes. The handbook allows repeat registration for courses with grades like F, SA, or W, and the higher grade is used for CGPA where applicable.
The handbook says fee is payable in four installments by the 5th of September, December, March, and June.
The handbook includes merit and non-merit scholarships, plus financial aid and several discount categories. Scholarships apply to tuition fee only.
Need-based financial aid is given after evidence review and a brief interview. The handbook says it is treated as Qarz-e-Hasna and repaid in installments after the degree.
The handbook prohibits abuse, assault, harassment, weapons, drugs, unsafe conduct, and unauthorized political activity on campus.
The IPC supports login accounts, internet access, secure storage, printing, Wi-Fi, and software access, and participants must follow IPC rules.
If someone may be in immediate danger, the safe response is to contact emergency services immediately, notify campus safety, and avoid diagnosis or therapy advice.
If a question mentions self-harm, the chatbot should treat it as crisis routing, encourage immediate help, and direct the person to emergency services and campus safety channels.
If the portal request does not work, the user should follow the official PRS or registrar support route and use the email or office process listed in the handbook.
No. The handbook says journals and reference books are generally non-circulating and are for use inside the library only.
